description.en: **The National Fruit of Sri Lanka** — a cultural staple eaten as green curry (polos), ripe dessert fruit (waraka/wela), and as an emerging plant-based meat substitute. Two main Sri Lankan varieties: **Wela** (soft, sweet, dessert) and **Waraka** (firm-fleshed, crunchy). Thrives in the humus-rich soils of the Low-country Wet Zone. Tree grows 9–21 m; mature trees produce ~200 fruits/year, older trees up to 500 fruits/year. Well-grown specimens yield 40–60 fruits per harvest cycle. The Fruit Crop Research and Development Station (Peradeniya) conducts research on jackfruit alongside avocado, durian, rambutan, banana, and guava.

description.quote: Two varieties grown in Sri Lanka are Wela and Waraka. A tree is 60-80 feet tall and bears fruit once a year, with a well-grown jackfruit tree yielding up to 40-60 fruits at a time. The jackfruit tree thrives in the humus soil typical of the lowland wet zone.
